"Sacrificed herself," huh... Sure. Yeah. That's the whole story...
可可利亚？她为了贝洛伯格…唔…我明白了。
Cocolia? In order to save Belobog, she... I see.
所以，下层区此前感受到的剧烈震动，就是那场战斗导致的，对吗？
So the massive tremors we felt earlier were a result of the battle, right?
Yes, it was a hard-won victory.
其实我们才是最大功臣…
We quite literally saved the world...
…谢谢你们。虽然很难想象你们究竟在上面经历了什么，但万幸结果是好的。
...Thank you. I can't even imagine what you've been through up there. I'm just glad everything turned out alright.
放心吧，她没事！布洛妮娅身体抱恙，希儿先带她去找铁卫了。
Don't worry, she's alright! It took a big toll on Bronya, so Seele took her over to the Guards.
那就好…你们都没事真是太好了。
Good... I'm so glad you're all okay.
可可利亚牺牲了，那个铁卫姑娘就会接过「大守护者」的位子吧？
Now that Cocolia is no more, I guess our Silvermane girl is about to become the next supreme guardian?
她是个意志坚定，言出必行的姑娘。有了她的帮助，下层区一定能重获新生。
She sees things through and doesn't go back on her word. The Underworld will thrive again under her watch.
嗯！我们也带了布洛妮娅的口信来哦——「封锁很快就会结束，下层区的人们很快就能自由地呼吸了」。
Mm-hmm! Oh, we brought you a message from Bronya! Ahem, "The blockade will soon be lifted. The people of the Underworld will breathe freely."
「自由地呼吸」…没错，那就是我们一直以来的愿望。
"Breathe freely..." That's been our wish all along.
三月，丹恒还有{NICKNAME}——能再请你们帮一个忙吗？我想把象征希望的好消息告诉大家，缓解紧张情绪。
March, Dan Heng, and {NICKNAME}... can I ask for your help again? I want to get this news out to everyone. It'll lift their spirits and bring them hope.
I can't wait to see their faces.
谢谢你们。该先去找谁就请各位决定吧，其余的交给地火就好。
Thank you. Choose whoever you like. Wildfire will take care of the rest.
下层区跟我们打过交道的人可真不少呢…{NICKNAME}，你觉得该先去找谁呢？
We've gotten to know a lot of Underworlders... {NICKNAME}, who do you think we should start with?
先通知大矿区的矿队吧。
Let's start with the mining team over at the Great Mine.
Let's start with Hook and The Moles.
我明白了，剩下的人就交由我们通知吧。如果一切顺利…这应该就是「地火」最后的任务了。
Roger that — you can leave the rest to us. If everything goes smoothly... this should be Wildfire's final mission.
辛苦你们跑这一趟了，大家应该都没事吧？
Oh, is everyone alright? Thanks for your help.
Maybe next time we can send a group notification...
哈哈。我觉得，当面传达的消息能承载更重要的意义吧。
Haha. Sometimes face-to-face communication is more meaningful, don't you think?
接下来，就是等待封锁解除的消息了。但愿布洛妮娅不会让大家等太久。
Now we just have to wait for the official news. I hope Bronya doesn't keep us too long.
说起来…守护者发生的变故，不知道史瓦罗和克拉拉有没有受到影响呢？
Huh... do you think Svarog and Clara felt the effects of our battle with the guardian?
我猜他们没事——史瓦罗对「地火」不客气，对克拉拉可是关爱有加。不过，如果你们真的担心…那不妨去机械聚落找找他们吧。
I think they'll be fine... Plus Svarog's devotion to Clara is even greater than his hostility to Wildfire. If you're worried, you could look for them over at the robot settlement.
大家好像都一脸慌乱的样子，希望旅店那边没出什么事……
Why is everyone in such a hurry? I hope nothing's happened at the hotel...
去看看情况吧，兴许还能帮上些忙。
We should take a look, maybe we can help.
我看到歌蒂了！怎么还有个人躺在地上？
I see Gertie! But who's that lying on the ground?
哎哟，这下坏了…小伙子？小伙子——你没事吧？
Oh my, this doesn't look good... Can you hear me? Are you okay?
应、应该还有气儿！歌、歌蒂大婶，别、别慌……
H—He's still breathing! T—Try not to p—panic Aunt Gertie...
这里发生什么事了？咦，这不是匹克嘛——莫非他又在路中间睡着了？
Is everything okay? Wait, isn't that... Peak? Don't tell me he fell asleep in the middle of the road again.
哎，居然是你们仨！你们怎么这么快就回来下层啦？
Oh, it's you three! Back in the Underworld so soon?
我这一阵正好在忙着拾掇大饭店的环境布置，匹克小伙是个热心肠，听说这事之后就马上跑来帮忙了。
Word must have gotten out that I was renovating the hotel today. Being the kind-hearted young man that he is, Peak came straight over and offered to help.
忙着忙着，整个旅店突然开始剧烈摇晃——我赶紧带着大家跑了出来，只有匹克小伙不小心被天花板掉下来的碎瓦片砸中了……
There we were hard at work, when all of a sudden the hotel started shaking... I got everyone outside as fast as I could, but I think Peak was hit by a ceiling tile...
确定他当时没在偷懒吗…
Are you sure he was "hard at work...?"
喂，他好像醒过来啦，快看！
Look! Seems like he's coming around.
应该没严重到那个程度。看，他已经醒了。
I... don't think that's necessary. Look, he's awake.
匹克小伙，你醒了？没事就好，没事就好！慢点慢点，别勉强自己……
Peak, are you awake? Oh, thank goodness you're alright! Easy now, take your time...
哎哟，我这老腰…刚才睡得正香呢，怎么突然就……
Argh, my back... I was having such a great nap, how did I...
你、你没受伤吧，哥们？我、我还是带你去娜、娜塔莎医生那儿看看吧……
A—Are you okay, bro? L—Let me take you to N—Natasha's clinic...
咦，你们看——匹克躺着的地方，好像有什么东西？
Hey, look where he was lying. What is that?
这是…好久之前的一张照片啊。我还以为老早就弄丢了，没想到居然……
...an old photo. I thought I lost this ages ago. How did it get...
这上面的人是…歌蒂大婶，还有老歌德？看上去好年轻呀！
Aunt Gertie, isn't that you in the photo? And Old Goethe, too! You look so young!
可不是吗。当时我那位远房大哥才刚接过宾馆的家业没多久，他亲笔写了封信邀请我去上层参观。没想到啊，这一别就是将近二十年……

Don't I know it. My distant cousin had only just taken over the family business at the time. He wrote me a letter inviting me to head up and take a look. It's been nearly <unbreak>20</unbreak> years already...
唉，也不知道这辈子还有没有机会再见他一面，再到宾馆大堂去喝杯茶……
*sigh* I remember chatting in the hotel lobby and drinking tea. I wonder if we'll ever get another chance to do that...
关于这个…我们有个好消息想传达给你。对吧，{NICKNAME}？
...Y'know, we've got some good news for you, haven't we, {NICKNAME}?
将胜利的消息分享给了歌蒂……
You tell Gertie about the victory...
…你、你们说的都是真的？布洛妮娅姑娘她…这、这简直就像在做梦一样！
...Is that true? Bronya, she... I—I can't believe my ears!
你和老歌德很快就能再见面了。
You and Old Goethe will have the kettle on the stove in no time.
我…哈哈哈，我就说嘛！失而复得，肯定预示着有好事发生！
I... Hahaha! I don't know what to say! Turns out things aren't always lost forever. *sigh* It feels like better days are finally on the horizon!
谢谢你们特意来告诉我这个好消息。我现在感觉浑身充满了干劲儿…等上下层的通路重新开放，肯定会有不少人来我这儿住宿吧？
Thank you all so much for coming to tell me the good news. I haven't felt like this in a long time... We'll get plenty of guests staying at the hotel again once the passageway is open!
可不能用这副模样迎接宾客…菜品数量也得再扩充一波才行。不多说了，马上开工！
But there's no way I'm letting them set eyes on the old place looking like this... Oh, and we're gonna need to expand the menu. Oh dear. Action stations!
开…开工？这么快？哎哟，我头好疼……
Action... stations? Already? Ooh, um — ow, oww...! My head...
麻烦你们转告娜塔莎医生，我这儿一切都好，不必操心——等大饭店翻修完成了，三位一定要再来光顾啊！
Please let Dr. Natasha know not to worry — we're all okay... You three will have to come and stay again once the hotel is fully renovated!
What was that shaking just now?
I thought the whole building was about to collapse...
The ground just kept on shaking...
No, it couldn't have been an earthquake...
Then could it be the end of the world?
I haven't even written my will yet!
What are you so worked up about?
It's not like we have much to will to anyone in the first place.
赶上了啊…我还以为你们要错过这个历史性的时刻了呢。
You made it... I thought you were about to miss out on a moment of history.
那怎么会！我们费了那么大力气才走到这步，怎么能错过最后的庆功宴？你说对吧，{NICKNAME}？
No way! After all our hard work, how could we miss out on the final celebration? Right, {NICKNAME}?
I'll go sit at the children's table.
虽然不知道会不会有所谓的庆功宴…但我想，虎克和克拉拉应该不会拒绝跟你们坐在一块儿的。
Haha, I don't know how much of a celebration we'll be having... but I know Hook and Clara will be pleased to sit with you.
空气中弥漫着自由的味道…
The smell of freedom's in the air...
对于我和奥列格，还有其他成年人来说，封锁结束意味着找回记忆中的生活方式，重新回到「寻常」中去，不必再为最基本的生存担忧……
For Oleg and I, not to mention the other adults, reopening the passageway means rediscovering old ways of living — a return to "normality..." Survival is no longer the top of the agenda...
但是对于那些在封锁年代里出生、长大的孩子们而言——这就是生命崭新的开始。
But for the children who were born into this era, who grew up in this isolation... this will be a new life altogether.
人一旦尝到了自由的甜头，就再难接受隔绝和闭塞了。我们这代人在童年经历的悲剧…绝对不能再在孩子们身上重演。
...Once you've tasted freedom, it's difficult to accept a life of separation and isolation. Those of us whose childhoods were marred by tragedy... we must strive to do better for the next generation.
抱歉，啰嗦的毛病又犯了。这是个值得纪念的日子，应该准备好笑脸来面对。
I'm sorry, here I am talking your ear off again. This is a day to remember. We should welcome it with smiles.
如果你愿意的话，就和周围的人们聊聊吧，他们每个人都激动得很呢。等大家都准备好了…我们就一起「回家」。
If you have time, talk to the folks here — every one of them will tell you about their excitement. Then, when you're ready... let's "go home."
终于…终于，我这个缆车道监护员又能重新上岗了！我还以为自己这辈子就要搭在矿道里了……
Finally... Finally I can get back to supervising the cable cars! I thought I'd spend the rest of my life in those mines...
啊，你们！是下层区的救星们！能为你们操作封锁结束后的第一班客运缆车，真是我莫大的荣幸！
Ah, it's you! The liberators of the Underworld! You're the first passengers we've had since being sealed off — it'll be my honor to get you to the surface in one piece!
呃…通融一下吧，{F#老妹}{M#老弟}？我也想去更新执照来着，可这下层区不是被封锁了十来年嘛……
Oh, uh, I'm afraid you might have to make an exception, {F#miss}{M#mr}. My license has expired, but then I haven't had the chance to go up and renew it for <unbreak>10</unbreak> years now...
Does this thing have a good safety record?
放心，放心！在我二十多年的职业生涯里，一共只出现过…呃…几乎从来没有出现过安全事故！
Relax, relax! In all my <unbreak>20</unbreak> years on the job, there were only ever, uh... Anyway, none of them were serious!
咳，不说没用的了——怎么样，你们准备好搭乘缆车了吗？
带孩子们去呼吸新鲜空气吧。
Time to take the kids out for some fresh air.
喂喂喂——兄弟姐妹、大爷大妈、男孩女孩们，都赶紧过来！客运缆车重新开始营运咯！
Brothers, sisters, ladies, gentlemen, boys, and girls of all ages — gather round! The cable car passenger service is about to restart!
没问题！你们不说话，我这第一班车绝对不开走！
No problem, this car won't be leaving without you!
怎么样？准备好搭乘缆车回上层去了吗？
呜…呜哇！这、这是…好大、好蓝的屋顶呀！
Whoaaaa...! Look at the big blue ceiling!
那不是屋顶，虎克…是「天空」。
That's not a ceiling, Hook... that's the sky.
这里…就是上层区吗？空气里的味道…和下层很不一样呢。
So this is... the Overworld? The air here smells... different.
哈哈哈，是少了些铁锈和地髓粉尘的味道吧？大口呼吸吧孩子们，这就是自由的气息啊。
Hahaha, less rust and Geomarrow particles floating around. Take a deep breath, children — this is what freedom tastes like.
没想到我还能活着回到行政区…在下面的这么多年里，还真是发生了不少事啊，娜塔莎。
...I never thought I'd live to see the Administrative District again. All those years... We've been through a lot, Natasha.
真是奇怪…坐在缆车上的时候，脑海里明明堆满了回来以后想做的事……
It's funny... The moment I boarded the cable car, my mind was filled with everything I wanted to do on the surface...
但现在…我只想找个地方坐下，发发呆，看着这忙碌的人流…就这么过一整天。
But now... I just want to sit down, zone out, and watch the people coming and going... and let the day just pass me by...
好好享受这片刻宁静吧，姑娘。这是咱们应得的。
Enjoy the peace and quiet, Natasha. We earned it.
十几年了，我终于能见着我的宝贝女儿了……
It's been more than ten years. I can finally see my little girl again...
她应该早就毕业了吧？希望她能找到份体面工作，行政事务或者法律顾问之类的……
She must have graduated by now. I hope she found a good job — government administration or legal consulting, perhaps...
琥珀王保佑…只要别去那些垃圾杂志社就好！她从小就爱看那些没营养的东西。
Please, Amber Lord, anything but those terrible magazine publishers! She's always liked to read those pointless things...
妈妈，上层区的小朋友们都穿什么衣服呀？
Mommy, what do children in the Overworld dress like?
妈妈也不知道呀…上层区和这里不一样，他们的时尚品味一直在变。不知道现在流行的是什么风格……
Mommy doesn't know either, sweety — the Overworld is different from here. Their fashion is always changing, I don't know what's popular these days...
那…阿丽娜上去了以后，会不会被其他小朋友笑话呀？
Then... will other children laugh at me when we go up?
傻孩子，才不会呢——咱们一上去，妈妈就带你去买最好看的新衣服。
Don't be silly, that won't happen — mommy's gonna buy you some pretty new clothes as soon as we're on the surface.
…谢谢妈妈，那我们就一言为定啦。
铁、铁卫…动作这、这么快…你们不觉得这里面有、有诈吗？
The Guards... they m—moved so q—quickly... don't you think it's a little f—fishy?
我…其实我也觉得很不可思议。但我见过那位布洛妮娅小姐…虽然交集不多，我还是觉得她值得信任……
I... I think it's hard to believe, but I've met that young lady Bronya. We didn't talk much, but she felt trustworthy to me.
我是不清楚那位新守护者的为人啦…不过听说那三个外来者和她关系很好。
I don't really know what kind of person the new guardian is, but I heard the three outsiders are on good terms with her.
那三位本事可大了，要是连他们都愿意相信铁卫，那这事儿也轮不到咱们质疑。
Those three are really capable people. If they believe the Guards, then there's no reason for us not to.
菲多拉说得对，他们在矿区救了很多人…我觉得他们值得信赖。
Fidora's right. They saved lots of people at the mines... I think they can be trusted.
Look, these are not regular people...
嘘…那可是铁卫的戍卫官！
Shhh! That's the captain of the Silvermane Guards!
The captain!? He has the gall to return?
铁卫的臭小鬼，大摇大摆……
Those Silvermane Gizzards, just prancing in here...
大守护者这不还没放弃咱吗？
The supreme guardian hasn't abandoned us, isn't it?
You dare to bring up the supreme guardian?
那个穿盔甲的哥哥好帅！
Those men in armor are so cool!
听虎克大人说，他们就是……
下层区的样子…和我记忆中的有所不同。
The Underworld... isn't exactly how I remember it.
感觉…周围人看我们的眼神，不是很友善啊。
...Feels like we're not getting many friendly looks, huh.
唷，瞧这阵仗！这小诊所哪儿见过这大场面啊？
Well, well, well! I don't think our little clinic has ever played host to an occasion like this!
哈哈哈，这么客气干嘛？哦，{NICKNAME}还不知道吧？之前守护者继任典礼的时候，我跟这位戍卫官老弟聊了两句，也算互相认识了——但旁边这位姑娘是？

Hahaha, Oleg will do, son. Oh, {NICKNAME}, you probably won't know... Our young Silvermane friend here and I had the pleasure of speaking to each other at a certain guardian's succession ceremony — we're not complete strangers. But as for this young lady...
我是…呃，前技术部的工程师希露瓦，也是杰帕德的亲姐姐。请多关照啦，首领！
The name's Serval. I'm, uh... an ex-engineer from the Technology Division and... Gepard's sister. Pleasure to meet you, chief!
这下可热闹了，不错不错！
Likewise, miss! The more the merrier!
正好{NICKNAME}也在，咱等会儿把该聊的聊完，你们几个一起留下来吃顿饭吧——先前一直没能好好招待，我和娜塔莎可惦记着呢。
And it's good to have you here again, {NICKNAME} — it bothered me and Natasha that we never arranged a formal welcome for you. Once we're done talking, you must all join us for a meal.
不胜荣幸。但正如您所说，我们还是先将要事谈妥为好。
We'd be honored. But to your point, Oleg — let's finish our discussions first.
这外面人来人往的，不大方便。咱们进诊所聊，娜塔莎也在等着呢！
Quite right. Let's head inside — too much commotion out here.
原来二位是姐弟关系…真是年轻有为。
...So she's your sister. The Landau family is full of talented young people I see.
不愧是贝洛伯格的戍卫官哪…想必是考虑到下层居民对铁卫会有所非议，这才想凭借开拓者的人望减轻阻力吧？

You're worried that Underworlders might still hold a grudge against the Silvermane Guards, so you want to show your solidarity with the Trailblazers to build some trust. Seems like a sound strategy to me.
您没说错，布洛妮娅大人始终在担心这件事。
Rebuilding trust between the two worlds has always been a priority for Lady Bronya.
在隔绝年代里，下层区的居民对银鬃铁卫想必是颇有微词。假使我们突然打着大守护者的名义接管下层…过程中一定会遇到不少阻力。

People here must have felt abandoned by the Guards after the passage was sealed off. If we were to simply "take over" the Underworld in the name of the supreme guardian... I can only imagine how people would react.
哈哈哈，分析得真是透彻！我算明白你为什么年纪轻轻就能当上戍卫官了！
Seems like you have a good handle on the situation. I see now why you made captain at such a young age, haha!
所以，容我总结一下：戍卫官先生的意思是，铁卫想要借此机会拉拢「地火」，重新在下层区树立铁卫的正面形象…对吗？
So... you're also saying the Guards want to work with Wildfire and use our reputation to win back people's support, correct?
…嗯。倘若不加任何修饰，这正是我们此行的目的。
...I wouldn't use those exact words, but that is more or less what we want to achieve.
If that's the case, I've got a question for you:
假如「地火」不愿与抛弃下层十余载的贵军合作…你们又会作如何反应？是要动用武力让我们屈服吗？
Since this offer comes from those who left us to our fate for more than a decade... how would you react if Wildfire refused? Hmm? Would you use force?
喂…这火药味儿好像有点浓啊……
Uh... she's not trying to be diplomatic about it, is she?
娜塔莎的气场完全不输杰帕德…
Starting to worry about your brother?
唔…老弟，你可别说错话啊……
Hmm... Choose your words wisely, Geppie...
前代大守护者的武断策略导致上下层区产生了隔阂，本该一心同体的贝洛伯格人陷入了相互的猜疑和记恨……

The barrier between the surface and the underground was the product of the former supreme guardian's misjudgment and the policies that resulted from it. The people of Belobog should have stood together, but instead we were made to blame and hate one another...
布洛妮娅大人致力于弥合裂口，重新树立铁卫的威信。但这并不意味着我们要用蛮横的方式「夺回」下层区。
Lady Bronya wants Belobog to heal, so the Silvermane Guards need to regain the people's trust. But we won't achieve that by kicking doors open and policing everyone around.
在我看来，「地火」作为民间组织，能够自发维持下层区十余年的秩序实属不易。诸位为了下层区的安定而倾注的决心，常人恐怕难以想象。

As a grassroots organization, Wildfire did an extraordinary job maintaining order for over a decade in the absence of the Guards. I can only imagine how much work you put in to protect the Underworld.
我向二位保证，铁卫此行绝非为抢功夺权而来。如若无法第一时间取得下层居民的信任，我们愿意从点滴做起，成为「地火」身边的助力，协助维持下层安定。

I can assure you that the Guards are not here to seize power. If it takes time for the people to trust us, so be it. We're willing to take small measures and serve as an auxiliary force to Wildfire to help maintain peace.
我们所做的一切皆是为了重建信任——重建团结、稳定的贝洛伯格城邦。
I hope you can see that everything we do, we do to rebuild trust and make Belobog work for all.
不愧是戍卫官，滴水不漏。
He thought of everything! That's a true captain for you.
难以置信，老弟居然能说出情商这么高的话……
I swear he never used to be this emotionally intelligent...
老弟原来还有这一面…果然我还是不够了解他啊。
I've never really seen this side of him before... the more you learn.
…不错的回答。奥列格，你怎么看？
...That's an answer I can accept. Oleg, what do you think?
哈哈哈，我看试探就到此为止吧。答案很明显了，我们的目标是一致的。
Haha, I think there's no need for the guessing game to continue. It's obvious we share the same goal.
重建团结、稳定的贝洛伯格…有这对姐弟帮忙，再加上布洛妮娅姑娘，听上去也不像天方夜谭嘛。
With the help of Bronya, Serval, and Gepard, we're one step closer to rebuilding a united Belobog.
我明白了。请转告布洛妮娅，「地火」会尽一切努力协调治安工作的交接。但考虑到下层区的复杂状况，还请做好应对各种意外的准——

I agree. Tell Bronya that Wildfire will work with the Silvermane Guards on security matters. However, things aren't simple down here — please also tell her to draw up plans in case the situation demands—
裂界里的怪物…朝镇子上涌过来了…！
糟了，好像出事了…你留在这儿安心照看病人吧，娜塔莎。外面的乱子就交给我们当兵的处理。
Not good... Stay here, Natasha — the patients need you. The soldiers and I will handle this.
…好，下层区的安全就托付给各位了。
（大家全都冲出去了…也去查看一下状况吧。）
(Everyone rushed outside... I should probably see what's happening.)
就不能进屋一口气说完？
Why don't you finish your story first?
对、对不起！我不知道各位在屋里……
I—I'm sorry! I didn't mean to scare you all...
发生什么事了？别慌张，慢慢说。
怪物…怪物入侵了矿区！求、求求你们——救救我的同伴！
Th—The monsters invaded the mine! Please, you have to save my friends!
要、要不然…他们、他们会被那些畸形的怪胎大卸八块的…！
Otherwise, those things will t—tear them apart!
别惊慌，银鬃铁卫一定会保障下层区居民的安全。你们从哪个方向来？
Don't worry, the Silvermane Guards are trained for these situations. Where do we find them?
大、大矿区！有特、特别大的一片矿脉的那个…！
The Great Mine! The one with the b—big ore vein...!
明白了——毋须担心，我马上安排铁卫前往设防、搜救难民。
Understood. I'll send my men to set up a perimeter and start the rescue operation right away.
奥列格先生——前往矿区前线搜救的任务就交给银鬃铁卫吧。
Mr. Oleg, please entrust the Silvermane Guards with the rescue.
磐岩镇的防御工事还有劳「地火」进行组织。请保证后方安全，同时接收从前线返回的伤员。
The defense of Boulder Town will need Wildfire's expertise. Watch out for ambushes and be ready to receive wounded soldiers.
哈！你这小伙还真有些顶头司令官的派头——收到指示，一定完成任务。
Ha, you're a natural-born commander. Understood, leave it to me, son.
对了，你手下那些新兵蛋子，对下层区的地形应该完全不熟悉吧？「地火」会抽派弟兄们陪你们上前线的。
Your troops will be unfamiliar with the layout of the mine — I'll send some of my men to be your guides.
…感激不尽。奥列格先生，我以「存护」之名向你保证，绝对会尽力挽救每一条生命。
...Thank you. In the name of the Preservation, I promise we'll do everything we can to save lives.
{NICKNAME}，希露瓦——我将带队先行赶往大矿区，你们尽快跟上。
I'll go on ahead and lead the troops to the mine. {NICKNAME}, Serval, catch up to us when you can.
…在那几个人面前，感觉我完全插不上话呢。
...I couldn't get a word in while they were talking.
恪尽职守的军人，还有奋力抗争的民间战士…跟他们比起来，我还真是一点儿也不摇滚啊。
A duty-bound soldier and a resistance leader... It's hard to be rock 'n' roll in front of people like that.
突然感觉…我这所谓的内心斗争有些儿戏，哈哈。
Y'know... maybe I need to put my "inner struggles" in a broader context, hehe...
每个人选择的道路都不同。
…说的也是。我会有这种想法，多半也是因为赋闲太久了吧？
...You're right. I've probably been idle for too long — it's easy to get stuck in your own head.
Rock 'n' roll means choosing your own way of living.
「自己」…嗐，我都快忘了自己到底在追求什么样的生活了。
My "own" way... Ha, I don't even remember what kind of life I want.
心理健康问题值得关注……
…不至于啦。习惯了站在台上的人可没那么容易消沉下去。
...I know. Anyway, don't worry, I'm doing fine.
只是…看着其他人在为了生存拼命，而我却在盘算着该怎么逃离这里…多少有些负罪感。
It's just... I see people fighting for their lives and here I am thinking about how to escape... I feel guilty about that.
…啊，不会吧——老弟他不会就是想靠愧疚感劝服我留下吧？！
Wait a minute, was that Gepard's plan all along? Use guilt to make me stay!?
我觉得杰帕德没那么心机…
He doesn't strike me as the type...
他也预料不到会有入侵事件吧…
Surely he couldn't have foreseen a monster invasion?
唔，说的也是。不管怎样，既然老弟坚持要带我一起下来，那我总得帮上点忙才行——得好好电电那帮怪物！
Hmm, that's true. Anyway, since he asked for help, it's time to give those monsters a shock!
你认识去大矿区的路，对吧？准备好了就去追上杰帕德吧！
You know the way to the Great Mine, right? Let's catch up with Gepard when you're ready!
就等你这句话了——我们走！
That's all you needed to say! Let's go.
你还需要准备一下吗？那等你准备好了就告诉我，我们一起出发。
Need to take stock? Just say the word — we'll head over together.
你认识去大矿区的路，对吧？准备好了就去追上杰帕德吧！
You know the way to the Great Mine, right? Let's catch up with Gepard when you're ready!
各、各位…求你们了，救救我的同伴！
原来是这样…前线的战况我了解了。当然，我们这边也没歇着，一直忙着照顾矿区那边送来的伤员呢。
I see... Thanks for bringing me up to speed. The clinic has been busy while you were gone. We've treated many people — soldiers and civilians alike.
总之，这次真的是辛苦你们了。只凭「地火」的力量，想平息这场骚动肯定会十分艰难。
All in all, I owe everyone a big thank you. It would've been much harder if Wildfire had operated alone.
多亏了杰帕德戍卫官，损失被限制到了最小程度。经此一役，下层区对银鬃铁卫的风评想必会改善不少。
Thanks to Captain Gepard, we managed to keep our losses to a minimum. I'm sure people will begin to appreciate the Silvermane Guards after this battle.
当然，也要谢谢你们的帮助——开拓者，还有希露瓦小姐。
Haha, how could I forget about you two? We couldn't have done it without your help.
嗐，别这么客气！他们两个才是大功臣。要不是他俩，我恐怕都要交代在矿区里了。
You're too kind, they deserve the real credit. I doubt I'd have come back in one piece without them.
听上去情况很糟啊…你没受伤吧，希露瓦小姐？要不要帮你诊断一下？
Sounds like you went through a lot of danger... Are you hurt, Miss Serval? I can have a look if you like.
没有没有，别担心！事情已经顺利解决啦。接下来，我们只要等他们回来……
I'm alright, don't worry! Everything worked out eventually. Let's wait for the rest of them to get back...
归来的杰帕德向娜塔莎汇报了矿区前线的种种状况……
Gepard returned and reported to Natasha what happened on the front line...
…总之，奥列格先生已经确认了矿区的灾情。
...Mr. Oleg has a pretty good handle on what's needed in the mine.
他现在正在镇上调度人员和物资，准备进一步的善后工作，所以暂时没办法回到诊所。
He's arranging for rescue teams and supplies to be sent over. He won't be back for a while.
看来骚乱已经圆满解决了…谢谢你们。
Looks like you guys have the situation under control. Thank you.
我还想和你们再多聊聊，但还有部分伤员没有得到妥善处置，我得继续照顾他们才行。
*sigh* I'd love to sit down and chat with all of you, but I still have a backlog of patients to see.
对了，诊所里有一对姐弟，听说是被你们亲手搭救下来的。你们有空的话，不如去确认下他们的情况吧？
Oh, there was a girl and her brother who said you saved their lives. You might want to check on them and see how they're doing.
娜塔莎小姐的提议不错。我们去慰问一下那对姐弟吧。
Natasha's right, we should make sure those children are alright.
C'mon, drink it up and you'll feel better.
可、可是…它好像在冒黑烟……
But... there's smoke coming out of it...
…啊，是你们！是在大矿区救了我们俩的——你、你们是上层区的「银鬃铁卫」大人，对吧？
Ah, it's you! You saved us from the monsters in the mine. You guys are... Silvermane Guards from the Overworld, right?
要不是各位出手相助，我们姐弟俩恐怕已经……
If you hadn't been there, we would've...
之后我会想办法回报各位的…！我很快就能找到合适的工作，然后就能……
I—I'll think of a way to repay you! I'll get a job, and—
那、那怎么好意思呢！要是爸妈还在，他们肯定也会想办法报答各位的……
Th—That's very kind of you... If Mom and Dad were still here, they'd want us to return the favor...
在生活上需要帮助也可以说。
Don't hesitate to let us know if you need anything.
没关系！吃穿用度还是没问题的……
Thanks! We can manage our daily expenses...
爸爸妈妈，很久以前就离开了。他们去了很远的地方，还没回来！
Mom and Dad went somewhere far away a long time ago — haven't come back yet!
...Mm. They'll come back one day, Zino.
…所以，你其实一直在独自照看弟弟？真是太辛苦了。
...So you're looking after your brother on your own? That must be hard.
不辛苦，这是身为姐姐的责任。只是现在的日子不太平，我总怕有一天……
Mm, not really — it's my responsibility as a sister. I'm just worried that with all these monster invasions, maybe one day I won't...
弟弟年纪还小，要他一个人在下层区生活下去…唉，我想都不敢想。
My brother is too young to survive in the Underworld. To think he might have to live on his own ...
…前任大守护者的决断导致了长达十余年的封锁，也酿成了诸多悲剧。
The misjudgment of the former supreme guardian isolated the Underworld for more than a decade. With that isolation, came tragedy...
但在布洛妮娅大人的引领下，银鬃铁卫会重新肩负起未尽的责任，重整秩序。到时候，你就不必再为自己和家人的安全担忧了。
But under Lady Bronya's leadership, the Silvermane Guards will atone for past mistakes and bring relief to the people. From now on, you don't need to worry about the safety of your family.
生命的分量太过沉重，况且你还需要肩负起弟弟的那一份…我知道这肯定十分不易。
Taking care of yourself is hard enough under these circumstances, let alone if you have a brother to look after. I know it can't have been easy.
你现在还小，所以需要姐姐的保护，这很正常。但等你长大以后，一定不要忘记姐姐为你付出的一切…你也要学会用自己的力量保护家人。
You're still young, so you need to stay under your sister's wing. But when you grow up, remember to use what you've learned to protect her.
你要努力成为姐姐的依靠。感觉无助的时候，你们至少还能对彼此敞开心扉。
Be there when she needs you, and listen to her when she feels vulnerable. She'll do the same for you.
我…我明白了！齐诺以后一定会保护好姐姐的！
O—Okay sir! I'll protect my sister when I grow up!
谢谢你的这些话，杰帕德大人。我和齐诺都会记在心里的。
Thank you, Captain Gepard. Zino and I will remember your words.
真是…气氛怎么又变得沉重了啊？要不今天就聊到这里？让姐弟俩好好疗养吧。
Y'know... we're pretty good at turning a happy reunion into a tear fest. Let's call it a day and give them some room.
老弟，还有精神吗？我想跟你聊两句。
If you're not too tired, Gepard, can I talk to you for a moment?
杰帕德…刚才的那番话，是你真实的想法吗？
What you said back there... did you really mean it?
当然。而且这绝对不是一时的感悟，而是我一直以来的想法。
Of course I did. And it wasn't some last-minute speech I cobbled together. It's how I've always felt.
——人不能总是单独面对问题，把挣扎永远藏在心里…要学会依靠他人，至少是亲近的人。
We can't always face problems alone and build walls to keep people out... We need to learn to rely on others — especially those we care about the most.
I know what you're up to, Geppie...
明明是想直接对我说的话，却还要借着别人拐弯抹角地说出来……
You made it sound like you were talking to those kids... but you were talking to me.
还好意思说我藏着掖着，明明你自己也一点都不坦率嘛！
Well, a Landau just called out a Landau.
计划有变——我要留在贝洛伯格，不上「星穹列车」啦。
Change of plan. I've decided to stay in Belobog.
姐姐，你…我只是想让你放下心里的负担，没有逼你留下来的意思。
Serval, I... I just want you to know that I would've supported your decision no matter what you chose. I wasn't trying to force you to stay.
放心吧，老弟。这是我经过深思熟虑以后才得出的结论。
I know that, Gepard. Don't worry, I've thought this one through for a change.
应该说，你让我彻底想明白了——我之所以会有离开的想法，更多是出于逃避的心理…那才是不成熟的体现。
What you said was the last piece of the puzzle for me. I suddenly realized that my decision to leave was just me trying to run away from my problems... Wasn't a mature decision.
我总是处理不好那些复杂的人际关系…不论是可可利亚，还是家族里的各种事。
I've always found it hard to handle complex relationships... be it Cocolia or my family.
遇到类似的问题，我第一时间想到的总是逃避。但这一点，从今天开始就要有所改进了！
The first thought I have when things don't work out is to run away. Starting from today, that's no longer the case.
毕竟…我不用再一个人应对这些问题了——对吧，老弟？
After all... I'm not doing this on my own, right?
多亏了你，我也看到了下层区人们的境况。我突然发觉，也许自己还能帮得上忙…也许我还能为这座城市做出些贡献。

If it weren't for you, I wouldn't have had any idea about the hardships people were going through in the Underworld. It made me feel like... maybe I could help a little, and make Belobog a better place.
杰帕德，{NICKNAME}——我还有最后一件事想拜托你俩。
Gepard, {NICKNAME}, I have one last thing that I might need your help with.
Let's hear it. Always glad to help.
…抱歉抱歉！我保证，这绝对是最后一次！
I'm so sorry! This is the last one, I promise!
我…想去看看星核。我想去看看可可利亚牺牲的地方。
I... want to see the Stellaron. I want to see the place where Cocolia sacrificed herself.
...Say no more. We'll go with you.
啊，在那之前，我还得先回一趟机械屋拿一件东西。{NICKNAME}，要不你就先去北边等我们吧？
Oh, but before that, I need to go back to the workshop and get something. {NICKNAME}, why don't you head north first and we'll catch up with you.
别怕——有老弟在身边，我们一路绝对安全。回见！
It's alright, I've got Gepard with me — we'll be safe. See you in a bit!
娜塔莎小姐的提议不错。我们去慰问一下那对姐弟吧。
Natasha's right, we should make sure those children are alright.
娜塔莎大夫，我们回来了…
尤利安，我这里有你的东西。
Julian, I've got your stuff here.
我听虎克大人说了，虎克大人带着你去了铆钉镇冒险，专程帮我找回了玻璃弹珠。
I heard Hook say that she took you adventuring in Rivet Town just to find my marbles.
我很感谢你，不过{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}别太得意了。要想当上鼹鼠党的二把手还是不够的，必须先过了我这关，我还没有打算把位置让出来呢！

Thank you very much, {F#miss}{M#mister}, but don't read too much into it. That's not enough for you to be the second-in-command of The Moles. You gotta get past me — and I ain't planning on giving up my position just yet!
…好、好了，快把我的东西交给我吧。
这么多全都是我的东西吗？谢谢你{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！我赢下来的玻璃弹珠，这个东西也是我的吗…「给<unbreak>50</unbreak>年后的尤利安」？
Is all of this my stuff? Thank you, {M#mister}{F#miss}! These glass marbles I won... Are these also mine? "For Julian, in <unbreak>50</unbreak> years' time"?
…啊，这个商业街后面铁门的钥匙，虽然现在已经没有人用得上了。真是谢谢你了！
...Ah, that's the key for the iron gates behind the business street. No one needs it anymore. Thank you so much!
{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}给了我这么多东西，我也得给你点什么，这样吧，我想把这本《冒险鼹鼠队》送给你。
You've given me so much stuff, {M#mister}{F#miss}. I have to give you something too — well, how about this copy of The Adventurous Moles?
哦？{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}也知道《冒险鼹鼠队》吗？
Oh? {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, do you know about The Adventurous Moles too?
每天晚上睡觉前我都要看一会儿《冒险鼹鼠队》，梦里全都是有趣的大冒险！
I read The Adventurous Moles every night before going to bed. My dreams are full of fun adventures!
等等，你不会是假喜欢吧…我问你，书里的三个主人公都是谁？
Wait, you're not pretending to like it, are you... let me ask you, who are the three protagonists in the book?
I confess I haven't read it.
我就知道，你一定要去看《冒险鼹鼠队》，书里全是有趣的大冒险。
I knew it, you have to read The Adventurous Moles. It is full of fun adventures.
他们是一个魔法学院的同学…
They are a trio that went to a wizarding school...
听上去很有意思，但是完全不对！你一定要去看《冒险鼹鼠队》，书里全是有趣的大冒险。
Hmm, interesting, but you're totally wrong! You should do better and read The Adventurous Moles. It is full of fun adventures.
我没见过脸皮这么厚的大人。你还是去看看《冒险鼹鼠队》吧。
Hmph, you are such a cheeky grownup. You should do better and read The Adventurous Moles.
那你一定要去看一看，书里全是有趣的大冒险！
Then you have to read it. It's full of interesting adventures!
I can't say I like it.
别装了，你其实根本没有看过《冒险鼹鼠队》对吧？不然，你告诉我书里的三个主人公都是谁？
Stop pretending. You haven't actually read it at all, have you? Tell me who the three protagonists are in the book.
I confess I haven't read it.
我就知道，你一定要去看《冒险鼹鼠队》，书里全是有趣的大冒险。
I knew it, you have to read The Adventurous Moles. It is full of fun adventures.
他们是一个魔法学院的同学…
They are a trio that went to a wizarding school...
听上去很有意思，但是完全不对！你一定要去看《冒险鼹鼠队》，书里全是有趣的大冒险。
Hmm, interesting, but you're totally wrong! You should do better and read The Adventurous Moles. It is full of fun adventures.
我没见过脸皮这么厚的大人。你还是去看看《冒险鼹鼠队》吧。
Hmph, you are such a cheeky grownup. You should do better and read The Adventurous Moles.
《冒险鼹鼠队》里有各种各样的冒险故事，…不过这本《冒险鼹鼠队》有些奇怪，只有前言和目录，你先看看这页前言。
There are all kinds of stories in The Adventurous Moles... But this edition is a bit strange. It's only got the preface and the table of contents. Here, read the preface.
作者在前言里说<color=#dbc291ff>「只要按照目录的提示，你就能找到对应的地点；只要找到对应的地点，你就能收集到对应的书页」</color>……
The author said in the preface <color=#dbc291ff>"If you follow the clues in the table of contents, you can find the corresponding locations, where you can collect the pages of the book."</color>...
它好像是在说这一本《冒险鼹鼠队》是有书页的，但是被藏了起来，但是想要找到书页的所在地，就必须得通过目录来发现线索。
It seems to be saying that this edition is meant to have book pages, but they are hidden somewhere, and we have to use the table of contents to find clues about those hidden pages.
你看目录里「消失的矿灯」那一章，下面写着<color=#dbc291ff>「在磐岩镇的大矿区里」</color>，这是不是在说书页就在那里呢？
Look at the chapter "The Missing Miner's Lamp." It says: <color=#dbc291ff>"In the Great Mine of Boulder Town..."</color> Is that where some of the missing pages are?
尤利安没找到过，也不知道是不是真的，不过{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}要是想了解《冒险鼹鼠队》的话，我可以都告诉你。
I haven't found anything, and I'm not even sure whether it's really true. But {M#mister}{F#miss}, if you'd like to know more about The Adventurous Moles, then I can tell you all about that.
《冒险鼹鼠队》有各种各样的大冒险，原始丛林的冒险、历史遗迹的冒险，还有外太空的冒险……
The Adventurous Moles has all kinds of great adventures. The Moles would often go into primitive jungles, lost cities, and even outer space...
Is there a jungle in Belobog?
大人一点儿想象力都没有吗？冒险的舞台当然可以是虚构的嘛。
Do grownups have no imagination? The adventures can be fictional, you know.
你们相信这书上写的内容吗？
Do you believe in the stuff written in the book?
你肯定以为儿童书里写的都是假的吧，哼，明明就是大人什么都不懂。
You probably think the things written in children's books are made up. Humph, you grownups just don't know better.
What is so interesting about The Adventurous Moles?
故事里的主人公们虽然都是小朋友，常常做到大人们无法达成的成就。另外，看《冒险鼹鼠队》的时候，我感觉自己也在冒险。
All the protagonists in the story are children, but they often achieve things that adults cannot do. And when I read The Adventurous Moles, I feel like I'm on an adventure, too.
作者就一直把读者叫作「第四只小鼹鼠」，所以我也是鼹鼠队的一员。
The author always calls the reader "the fourth little mole," so I am also a member of The Moles.
冒险故事就是会有吸引力。
Adventure stories are indeed fun to read.
哎，好想带着应急食物去魔法与剑的世界里大冒险啊……
Oh, how I wish I could go on an epic adventure in a fantasy world of magic and swords, with my very own emergency food!
鼹鼠党的名称是这么来的吗？
Is that how you guys got the name "The Moles"?
我懂了，这是小屁孩儿的爽文。
I see. So it's a fairy tale for naughty kids.
Humph, you big grownup dum-dum don't know anything.
Who's the lead in The Adventurous Moles?
三个小鼹鼠你都不知道吗？
Don't you even know the three little moles?
他们分别是独立自主比男孩子更厉害的贝琪，擅长发明创造的卡宾，还有身强体壮的帕瓦！
There's Becky, who is so independent and much cooler than boys; Carbine, who is good at invention and crafting; and Pava, who is strong!
{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，你看<color=#dbc291ff>目录上提到的大矿区</color>，也许在那里会有什么发现呢？
{M#Mister}{F#Miss}, look, the <color=#dbc291ff>Great Mine</color> is mentioned in the table of contents. Maybe there's something there?
哇哦！没想到真的让{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}找到了《冒险鼹鼠队》的书页。
Wow! {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, I never expected you to actually find one of the pages of The Adventurous Moles.
说不定{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}<color=#dbc291ff>凭借着目录的提示，还能找到剩下的书页！</color>
{M#Mister}{F#Miss}, maybe you can <color=#dbc291ff>track down all the rest of missing pages with clues from the table of contents!</color>
What benefits would there be if I find all the pages?
你可以收集齐之后给我看看呀。
You go find those pages so that you can let me read them.
…我知道你想说，这根本不算是什么好处。不过只想着要好处，大人也太急功近利了吧。
...But I know what you mean. There's nothing in it for you at all. Only thinking of benefits — grownups are so greedy for cheap thrills.
I don't know where to look. Help me.
{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}要是再求求我，我说不定可以再考虑一下。
If you ask me again nicely, I might think about it.
好吧，我就大方告诉你吧。
Fine, I'll be generous this time.
我也找不到。不然我早就找齐了，哪儿还有你的事。
But I don't know where to look either. Otherwise, I would have found them myself. What would I need you for?
哼，小屁孩儿到一边玩儿去。
哼，我本来也没有打算告诉你。
Hmph, you big grownup dum-dum. It's not like I wanted to tell you in the first place.
说真的，我现在有些期待了，等{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}全部收集齐了之后再找我吧。
But seriously, {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, I'm actually starting to look forward to reading all those pages now. Please come and find me whenever you've found them all.
嗯？{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}不会想问我如何找到剩下的书页吧？既然{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}诚心诚意地发问了，那就别怪我胡说八道了。
Hmm? So you wanna know where you can find the rest of the missing pages? Well, since you asked nicely, I'll tell you all about it. But don't come blame me if I get anything wrong.
《冒险鼹鼠队》的目录上还有3个章节，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}想问哪个？
There are still three more chapters listed in The Adventurous Moles's table of contents. Which one do you want to ask about?
「在边缘通路的露天咖啡馆入口附近」。
"At an open-air café in Backwater Pass..."
边缘通路…是在上层区吗？露天咖啡馆又是什么东西……
Backwater Pass, hmmm...? Is that up in the Overworld? And what is an open-air café...
…你问我，我也不清楚，你自己去找找就知道了。
...Don't even try to ask me more. I wouldn't know. You probably should go there to take a look for yourself, and you'll find out soon enough.
「在铆钉镇一处摆放着货架的洞窟里」。
"In a Rivet Town where storage shelves are placed..."
在铆钉镇、放着货架的洞窟…是不是娜塔莎医生的药房呢？就在孤儿院的旁边。
"Rivet Town... a cave with storage shelves..." Could that be Dr. Natasha's pharmacy, next to the orphanage?
…你问我，我也不清楚，你自己去找找就知道了。
...Don't even try to ask me more. I wouldn't know. You probably should go there to take a look for yourself, and you'll find out soon enough.
「铁卫禁区深处通往雪原的战壕工事」。
"...the trenches deep in the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone, on the way to the Snow Plains..."
铁卫禁区…那是银鬃铁卫的基地吗？通往雪原的话，应该是在基地最深处吧。
The Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone... is that their base? If it leads to the Snow Plains, would that be right in the deepest part of the base?
…你问我，我也不清楚，你自己去找找就知道了。
...Don't even try to ask me more. I wouldn't know. You probably should go there to take a look for yourself, and you'll find out soon enough.
I don't have any other questions.
那好吧。要是{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}收集齐《冒险鼹鼠队》记得来告诉我呀。
Alright. {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, remember to come find me once you've found every page.
我收集齐了《冒险鼹鼠队》…
I collected all of The Adventurous Moles...
WOW! Did you really find them all?
…咦？你这不是还有一页后记没收集到吗？
... Hey? Isn't there a page of postscript you haven't found yet?
后记不知道到哪里去找。
I don't know where to find the postscript.
我这里刚好有后记，喏，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}你就拿上吧。
I happen to have the postscript here. There you go, take it.
这样你才算是收集齐了一整本《冒险鼹鼠队》，真不愧是{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，我可没有这样的耐心。
Now you have collected the whole book of The Adventurous Moles, great job. You did great, {M#Mister}{F#Miss}, I myself wouldn't have the patience to find all these missing pages.
Where did you find the postscript?
这不是我找到的，鼹鼠党每天都在大冒险，我可没有时间去找《冒险鼹鼠队》。
I didn't find it. I've been going on adventures with The Moles every day. I don't have time to look for The Adventurous Moles.
这页后记是娜塔莎大姐姐交给我的，你正好没有，我就交给你了呗。
The postscript was given to me by big sister Natasha. You happened to be missing it, so I gave it to you.
哼！小孩子就是没有耐心，这是世间的常识。
Hum! Everyone knows that kids are all impatient.
再说了，这个作者写书写得很慢，下一本书又不知道要等到什么时候了。
Besides, the author writes very slowly. I don't know how long I'll have to wait for the next book.
{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，我有个问题。越是让人期待的作品，越是让人等得很久。是不是期待让时间变慢了呢？
I have a question for you. The more I look forward to something, the longer I have to wait. Is time being slowed down by expectations?
期待确实会让等待变得更长。
哎，那我只能让自己别那么期待了。
Hey, then I should look forward to it less.
... Can I even manage to do that, I wonder?
No, the author is just being lazy.
原来如此，看来是我给大人找借口了。
I see. It seems that I just made an excuse for adults.
你不想看看整本书的故事吗？
Don't you want to read the whole book?
…想看是想看的，但是只有我自己能看到的话，太没有意思了。
...I do want to read it, but it's boring if it's just me by myself.
对了，既然{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}已经收集齐《冒险鼹鼠队》的书页，不如将他们交给出版社吧，这样贝洛伯格所有的小朋友都能看到了。

Oh, {M#mister}{F#miss}, since you've already collected all the pages for The Adventurous Moles, why don't we bundle them up and give them to a publisher? That way, all children in Belobog can read it.
Yet even more errands to run...
要是{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}把书稿交给出版社肯定会有报酬的。
{M#Mister}{F#Miss}, you'll definitely hit a payday if you hand the manuscript to a publisher.
快去吧！我和鼹鼠党的小伙伴都等着新书的好消息。
Go on, then! The Moles and I can't wait to see the new book getting published.
我收集齐了《冒险鼹鼠队》…
I collected all of The Adventurous Moles...
嗯？你是说《冒险鼹鼠队》？不好意思，你能交给我看看吗。
Huh? Do you mean The Adventurous Moles, the book? Could you let me have a look at it first, please?
...I never thought this would be real.
我小时候还是挺喜欢看《冒险鼹鼠队》的，特别是里面描写的什么「热带雨林」、「沙漠」…就好像这些地貌是真实存在的。
I loved reading The Adventurous Moles when I was a kid, especially the bits about the rainforest and the desert... As if those things really existed.
In the real world, there's only snow outside.
但是人们的想象是会超越风雪的嘛……
But people's imaginations can far surpass the wind and the snow...
哈哈哈哈，也有很多读者像你一样，都以为这些描述是真的。
Hahaha, there are so many readers who are just like you, believing these writings to be real-life events.
哎，这也是儿童文学的魅力嘛。
But, hey, that's the charm of children's stories.
我想把《冒险鼹鼠队：隐形的宝藏》整理之后出版。虽然你不是作者，我没法支付你稿费，不过这是我的一点心意，请你收下。
I do want to put together and publish The Adventurous Moles: Hidden Treasure. I know you're not the author, so I can't pay out any royalties to you, but here's a little something for you. Take it.
买了，很好看，我一晚上就看完了。
Yup, it's great. I read the whole thing in one night.
然后，作者什么时候再出下一本呢？作者是不是挖坑不填坑啊，作者不会是跑路了吧？
Well then, when's the author putting out the next book? Don't tell me they won't tie up all the loose ends? No way!
读者从来都是不好伺候的，大人连这都不懂吗？
Readers always have been fickle. How can grownups not understand this?
作者赶快给我出下一本！
We want another book out NOW!
没错没错，等我长大了，就太晚了！
Correct, it'll be too late by the time I grow up!
虽然我长大了也会接着看，但是我不管，作者你还是快点出！
Even though I'll keep reading it once I grow up... I don't care. Just give us a new book, author!
这一次多亏了{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，大家都很感谢你。要是哪一天{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}有空了，也把你的冒险故事讲给我们听吧。
Thanks so much, {M#mister}{F#miss}. Everybody is super grateful. When you have the time, tell us all about your adventurous stories.
垃圾箱沉默不语，庞然巨物温柔地注视着你。
The dumpster remains silent. This hunk of junk gazes gently at you.
当你来到这座垃圾箱面前时，你感到一阵解脱。结束了，贝洛伯格冬日在呼吸声中说，这就是最后一个。你的感觉已经无关紧要，创造这群垃圾箱的人已经完成了最终的创作。

You feel a sense of relief when you stand before this dumpster. "It's all over," breathes Belobog's winter, "This is the last one." Your feeling no longer matters. What matters is that the ones who created these trashcans have fulfilled themselves.
垃圾箱静静矗立，传来隐隐恶意。
The dumpster stands silently, yet a hint of sinister comes through.
一位科学家曾经说过：当垃圾箱盖紧紧关闭的时候，它同时处于有垃圾和没有垃圾的叠加态。
A scientist once said, "When a dumpster is shut tight, it exists in a superposition of states where it is both devoid of trash and full of trash at the same time."
你依稀记得某部电视剧里，律师钻进这样的垃圾箱，等待目标的犯罪证据从天而降。
You vaguely recall a television series where a lawyer hid inside a dumpster such as this and waited for an incriminating piece of evidence to appear out of thin air.
你依稀记得某部电视剧里，街头小子钻进这样的垃圾箱，紧紧捂住自己的嘴巴，等待带着枪的追兵离开。
You vaguely recall a television series where a homeless kid hid inside a dumpster like this, tightly covered his mouth, and waited for his armed pursuers to leave.
你依稀记得某部游戏里，身手高强的主角钻进这样的垃圾箱，当敌人走近时，他如同黑夜将对方掩藏，没有发出任何声响。
You vaguely recall a video game in which the protagonist hid inside one of these dumpsters and quietly took out the enemy when they approached.
你依稀记得某部游戏里，落魄的警官付不起当晚的房费，他在店主的奚落笑声中钻进这样的垃圾箱——然后跳了个成就。

You vaguely recall a video game in which a destitute police officer could not afford to pay for the night's room and dove into a dumpster like this amidst the taunting laughter of the shopkeeper — and apparently got an achievement out of it.
你依稀记得某部游戏里，这样的垃圾箱里能捡到关键的道具，用来解开附近的谜题，前往下一片区域。
You vaguely recall a video game in which a key item can be found in dumpsters like this to solve a puzzle and unlock the next area.
你不明白自己对垃圾箱的执念从何而来，但上述作品应该脱不了干系。
You don't understand where your obsession with dumpsters come from, but it must have to do with the aforementioned work.
塞得满满当当的巨大垃圾箱。各种垃圾混杂在一起，产生了奇妙且酸臭的化学反应。但地下的居民们毕竟有一点可以聊以自慰：他们还不需要进行垃圾分类。

A huge dumpster filled to the brim. The mixture of all kinds of trash results in a putrid odor. However, those who live in the Underworld have some consolation: They don't need to go through the trouble of sorting their garbage.
巨大的垃圾箱。如果你得了感冒，也可以把它当胶囊旅馆来用。
A huge dumpster. If you caught a cold, you can even use it as a capsule hotel.
垃圾箱的盖子敞开着，似乎在催促你把失去意识的巡逻士兵丢进去。
The opened lid of the dumpster seems to be urging you to leave the unconscious bodies of the patrolling soldiers inside.
世界上有两种英雄，一种在被看见前躲进垃圾箱，另一种在被看见前把别人扔进垃圾箱。
There are two types of heroes in this world: One of them hides in the dumpster before they were seen, and the other hides bodies in the dumpster before they were seen.
这箱子身躯硕大，内部却空空荡荡。人生也不外如是。
This trashcan is huge, but its inside is empty. Life is no different.
你看到一只可怜的铁皮桶，它的腰部分明遭受过重击。似乎有人路过时，将澎湃的情感宣泄在这无辜的垃圾桶上。
You see a pitiful trashcan made of tin, with a torso clearly dented from trauma. It seems like someone took their pent-up emotions on it whilst passing by.
两只垃圾桶紧紧地并排站着，仿佛世界上没有任何力量能将它们分开。
The two trashcans stood tightly side by side as if no force in the world could separate them.
两只垃圾桶紧紧地并排站着，它们的影子融在一处，像在拥抱。
The two trashcans stood tightly side by side, their shadows meld together as if they were locked in an embrace.
两只垃圾桶紧紧地并排站着，如果一只倒下，另一只必不会独站。
The two trashcans stood tightly side by side. If one of them falls, the other will surely follow.
两只垃圾桶紧紧地并排站着，或许这就是世界上最美好的事情：有另一个自己坚定地站在你的身边，即使只是一只垃圾桶。
The two trashcans stood tightly side by side. This might be one of the most beautiful things in the world, to have another self standing by your side, even if it is just a trashcan.
只要两只垃圾桶站在一起，它们就可以面对宇宙中的所有苦难。
As long as trashcans stand together, they can weather all difficulties in the universe.
垃圾桶冷冷凝视脚边喘息的垃圾桶：「我说过的，塔塔洛夫。」它说，「我说过总有一天，你会翻倒在我的脚边，到那个时候，我会狠狠地嘲笑你。风水是会轮流转的。」

The trashcan glared coldly at the other trashcan gasping for air at its feet. "I told you, Tatalov," it said. "I told you that you will one day collapse beside me. When that time comes, I will laugh to your dying face. Such is the wind of change."
污黑的液体从垂死的垃圾桶的身下流出，蜿蜒向前。它向上望，盯着垃圾桶冰冷的双眼。十五年前，当垃圾桶还是一个小垃圾桶的时候，它就见过这样的眼神。

A goo of darkness dripped out of the dying trashcan's form and continued to snake its way forward. It looked up at the cold eyes of the other trashcan. Fifteen years had passed since it had last seen that gaze, when the standing trashcan was still tiny.
「是我…」垂死的垃圾桶艰难地说，「是我养大了…你……」一阵疼痛摄住了它，那一瞬间，它意识到，自己体内的某个垃圾袋破了，绞痛令垂死的垃圾桶呜咽起来。垃圾桶满意地笑了。

"It's me..." the dying trashcan bade, "I was the one... who raised you..." A wave of sharp pain enveloped it. At that moment, it realized that a certain trash bag had burst. The shooting pain coaxed a whimper out of the dying trashcan while the other one snickered satisfactorily.
它蹲下，在垂死的垃圾桶耳边低声说出一个名字。「你是——」垃圾桶睁大眼睛，「可是——不…不可能……」最后一个字凝结在它嘴边，就像垃圾桶眼角噙住的泪滴。

It crouched down and whispered a name in the ear of the dying trashcan. "You—" The trashcan's eyes were wide open. "But— No, you can't be..." Its final words curdled inside its mouth, just like the teardrop hanging from the corner of its eye.
封路的栅栏总留给你对未来的无限遐想。
The road closure barrier always leaves you with an infinite reverie about the future.
或许未来的某一天，铁栅栏会从这个世界消失，敞开前方的道路。
Maybe some day in the future, the metal barriers will disappear from this world, opening doors toward a future possibility.
这是路灯，这样的说明应该就足够了。
This is a street lamp, this description should suffice.
你为什么要调查一根路灯？它又没有任何作用。
Why are you investigating a street lamp? It doesn't do anything.
把时间花在那些看上去有点奖励的东西上吧，这只是个路灯。
Spend some time on the things that might have a reward, this is only a street lamp.
这根路灯不会掉落道具和素材，也不提供新的任务。
This street lamp will not drop items or material, and it will not unlock a new mission.
路灯，它在昏暗的地下扮演太阳的角色。
A street lamp, it plays the part of a zeitgeber in the dim underground.
这只邮筒蜷缩在角落里，似乎并不希望被发现。「还是别去打扰它了吧。」通情达理的你知道，既然以社交性著称的人尚且需要一点私密空间，何况邮筒呢。
This mailbox is tucked away in the corner, trying not to be noticed. "Better not disturb it." You get the sense that even the most sociable person would need some personal space, let alone a mailbox.
书写信件需要时间，寄送信件也需要时间。庞大的时间成本，才使得通信如此弥足珍贵，有谁会对廉价的玩意趋之若鹜呢？只是动动手指而已——比如朋友发来的短信，根本懒得回复不是吗。

Composing a piece of mail requires time, and so does mailing it. Written mail's value comes with its temporal investment. Who would derive excitement from that which is of low-value? It's just a tap and a swipe, like the text message from your friend you didn't bother to reply.
开采工具散乱地堆放在诊所门口。
Mining tools are scattered around the clinic's entrance.
你看见十字镐，因其尖头一端状如鹤嘴。也被叫做鹤嘴锄。它是园艺和土石采掘中常用的工具。
You see a pickaxe. Its pointed end resembles the beak of a crane, so it is also colloquially called a cranebeak. A common tool used for gardening and mining.
采掘用具横七竖八地躺在路边，睡相十分奔放。
Mining tools are laid haphazardly on the roadside.
采矿工具的木柄上都有一片变色的区域，这是其主人辛苦劳动的证明。
There are discolored patches on the wooden handles of these tools, telling the tale of their owner's hard work.
你试图用单手拿起一柄铁锹，反而被其巨大的重量拉扯着失去了重心。
You try to pick up a shovel with one hand and are immediately thrown off-balance by its weight.
有些工具的木柄上刻着主人的名字，这些工具普遍得到了精心呵护。
Some of these tools had their owner's name carved on them. They were probably very well taken care of.
你看见一柄久经风霜的铁锹，锹头已经被锐利的岩石啃得伤痕累累了。
You see a weathered shovel, the head of which has been gnawed and scarred by the sharp rocks.
你看见一柄看似崭新的十字镐，两端镐头几乎未有撞击开掘的痕迹。
You see a pickaxe in near-pristine condition, both of its points barely having any scratches on them.
结晶矿石在板条箱中闪着荧荧光芒。
Crystalline ores sparkle inside the crate.
木制的板条箱中装着矿石，这是一种名为「地髓」的优质能源。
The wooden crate carries a kind of ore called Geomarrow, an excellent energy source.
「地髓」——顾名思义，是行星大地的髓液，对于雅利洛-Ⅵ和贝洛伯格而言是无比珍贵的宝藏。可矿石总有开采殆尽的一天——到那时候会发生什么？没人知道，没人想知道。

Geomarrow, as the name suggests, is the essence of the planet. To Jarilo-VI and Belobog, it is an invaluable treasure. However, ores will run out one day, and what will happen then? No one knows, and no one wants to know.
地髓矿石被切削得方方正正——又或者它天生就长得如此端庄大方。
The Geomarrow ore was cut into a cuboid, or maybe it was formed into this elegant shape naturally.
地髓，这颗行星现今为数不多的可用能源。在上下封锁以后，只有这种矿石依然有权返回地上。
Geomarrow, one of the few still-usable energy sources on this planet. After the blockade was implemented, only this type of ore was allowed to return to the Overworld.
聪明的人会用这些矿车整个活，但不知道他们整的活聪不聪明。
Smart miners will use these mine carts to make their work easier, but it remains up for debate whether it makes their work smarter.
你有种预感，这东西在不久以后的将来，会给你创造麻烦。
You have a feeling that this thing will cause you problems in the future.
海报似乎在邀请你加入「搏击俱乐部」。
The poster seems to be tantalizing you to join the Fight Club.
所谓「搏击」，无非三种功法：拳法、掌法、指法。如果搏击运动对你来说还太沉重，不妨先从剪刀石头布开始练起。

When people talk about fighting, they are referring to one of three things: Punching techniques, palm strikes, and finger strikes. If fighting sports are too heavy for you, you can start with rock, paper, scissors.
搏击，又称搏斗。它不是斗殴，而是一种健康向上，有益身心，弘扬体育精神的竞技项目。
Fighting, also known as brawling. It is not just a scuffle, but a healthy, physical and mental athletic sport that promotes sportsmanship.
鼓风机呜呜地吹着，可能这就是所谓的「鼓劲」吧。
The blower whirrs and drums. Maybe they were trying to cheer for you.
你看见沙袋，它的工作就是挨揍。
You see a sandbag, its job is to take a beating.
你看见沙袋，它一天<unbreak>24</unbreak>小时在岗，任劳任怨，伤痕累累，一分钱工资都没有，还要遭人白眼。你希望它能站起来。
You see a sandbag that works <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7. It never complains, is full of damage, was never compensated, and still had to be looked down on. You hope that it can stand up on its own.
从沙袋上你能学会的事：重是有好处的，当别人攻击你时，他的手会疼。
What you learned from observing the sandbag: It pays to be heavy. When people are attacking you, their hands will hurt.
从沙袋上你能学会的事：如果一声不吭，就没人知道你有多痛。
What you learned from observing the sandbag: If you don't make a sound, no one would know how much pain you're in.
从沙袋上你能学会的事：你受的苦楚，多半只是另一个人成长的经验值。
What you learned from observing the sandbag: Your pain will likely be another person's experience.
从沙袋上你能学会的事：遭到打击时，不妨随势摇摆，再利用惯性反击。
What you learned from observing the sandbag: When assaulted with blows, try to move with the force and counterattack using momentum.
从沙袋上你能学会的事：倒下就要倒得干脆，旋转跳跃并没有人欣赏，只是浪费时间。
What you learned from observing the sandbag: Commit to your choices, even if you're falling. Stylistic theatrics is just a waste of time that no one appreciates.
这面墙上贴满了各式海报、招贴、告示。其中一张吸引了你的注意，它仿佛很新，纸面上却留下了许多污黑的指纹。
All sorts of posters, invitations, and notices are plastered upon this wall. One caught your attention. It looks new, but there are an abundance of dirty fingerprints on it.
很显然，许多人都曾被这份《拳手招募单》激起了内心的熊熊烈火。
It is clear that for many people, this Boxer Recruitment Form had ignited their fighting spirit.
斑驳的墙面上散乱地张贴着各类纸张。光是看一眼这面墙，你的耳朵便本能地感到嘈杂。
All forms of papers have been chaotically taped on this patchy wall. With just one look at it, you can almost hear the cacophony that saturated the space.
在贝洛伯格你绝无可能看到这样一面墙：七零八落的纸张仿佛苔藓般爬满其表面。上层人的墙古板优雅，而地下的墙则充斥着独特的烟火气。

You will never be able to see a wall like this in Belobog: Sheets of paper occupy every inch of its surface like moss. The walls of the Overworld are classical and stately, while those in the Underworld exude a unique, quaint poignancy.
你看见一张极具侵略性的海报。从用色、到文案、再到那沙包一样大的铁拳图案，全都极具侵略性。

You see an extremely aggressive poster. Everything about it, from its color choice, to its provocative text, to the fist illustration as large as a sandbag, they all ooze strongly of combative passion.
这座古老的垃圾箱沉静、严肃、不怒自威，令你联想起一位老成持重的长者。许多年来，它就这样伫立在地下街边，见证沧海桑田，世事变迁。
This old dumpster stands silently, carrying its own resolute gravitas that reminds you of an elder of vast wisdom. It stood on the side of the street for years, witnessing its rise and fall.
呃，你可能没注意看刚才的旁白：这座垃圾箱长者已经在本地伫立了很久，它就像一位长者，你必须尊重一位长者的智慧。
Uh, you may have missed the narration just now: This elderly dumpster had been standing in this spot for years. You must respect its years of accumulated wisdom.
I have no idea what you're saying...
不——要——打开这座垃圾箱，让它就这样安静地、拥有尊严地待在那儿，好吗？
Do. Not. Open. This dumpster up. Just let it stand in its place quietly, respectably, alright?
Are you trying to stop me?
你是不可阻挡的，好吗？你的意志是绝对的。只是给你一个建议：不——要——打开这座垃圾箱，让它就这样安静地、拥有尊严地待在那儿。

You are unstoppable, alright? Your will is absolute. This is just a piece of advice: Please refrain from uncovering the dumpster's contents and just let it stand here with the silence and dignity it deserves.
I still want to open it, I've made up my mind.
没问题，你想做什么都行：瞧！垃圾箱盖在你绝对的意志下开启，里面空空如也，什么都没有，但看看好的一面！你强力地展示了权威。玩游戏的时候，这是最重要的！

Of course, you can do whatever you want: Look! The dumpster lid opens under your absolute will. There is nothing inside, absolutely nothing, but look on the bright side! You have imposed your might unto it. This is the most important aspect in any game!
Sure, I will take back my insistence.
很明智，你是一个从善如流的人，垃圾箱长者不动声色地向你致意。你感到那沉重的历史积淀从它向外扩散，在土地的深处回荡——回荡——

That is wise. You are an open-minded person who readily accepts opinions of others, and Elder Dumpster silently tip its figurative hat at you. You feel the hefty history of its existence emanate outward in every direction, echoing in the depths of the earth.
你离开了它。你走得很坚决，但即使相距甚远，你仍感到那沉重的历史积淀感，从垃圾箱长者向外扩散，在土地的深处回荡——回荡——

You leave it alone. You walk away resolutely, but even with the distance you have put between you, you still feel the heft of its history emanating outward from the dumpster, echoing in the depths of the earth.
你向垃圾箱长者行了个礼，而它回报给你的是深沉的尊严——足足有两个垃圾桶那么大。
You bow at Elder Dumpster, to which it responds with a deep dignity, it almost feels like it is the size of two dumpsters.
这座垃圾箱给你一种特别的感觉，这与它的位置、形象与气味无关，是一种小心翼翼的避世气质，兼有一丝难言的温馨。垃圾箱的箱口贴着一张皱巴巴的纸条，上面写着<unbreak>81</unbreak>。

This dumpster evokes a special emotion in you. Unrelated to its location, appearance, and olfactory triggers, it is a sense of hiding from the world as well as a hint of preternatural coziness. A wrinkly piece of paper is stuck on the lid of the dumpster with the number "<unbreak>81</unbreak>" on it.
你看见一间简陋的居室：一条起皱泛黄的铺盖，麻袋做成的枕头，两瓶已开封的面包味汽水，一双矿工靴，一本书，书页已经烂了，你看不清上面写了什么。以及，一把小刀。

You see a shabby room with a set of wrinkled yellowish bedding, a pillow made from a gunny sack, two opened bread-flavor soda, a pair of miner's boots, a torn out book whose words can no longer be distinguished, as well as a knife.
看来有人在这只垃圾箱里筑了个简陋的小窝。
Looks like someone used this dumpster as their humble living quarters.
你做得很对，这座垃圾箱是某个人的私有领域。这两下轻轻的敲打——咚，咚咚——意义重大，它意味着尊重。这世上，体面的人们也并不真正拥有尊重。

Well behaved! This trashcan is someone's private zone. *rat-a-tat-tat* Knocking on the wall before entering represents your respect. In fact, however, not every decent person can really win others' respect.
你看见一间简陋的居室：一条起皱泛黄的铺盖，麻袋做成的枕头，两瓶已开封的面包味汽水，一双矿工靴，一本书，书页已经烂了，你看不清上面写了什么。以及，一把小刀。

You see a shabby room with a set of wrinkled yellowish bedding, a pillow made from a gunny sack, two opened bread-flavor soda, a pair of miner's boots, a torn out book whose words can no longer be distinguished, as well as a knife.
看来有人在这只垃圾箱里筑了个简陋的小窝。
Looks like someone used this dumpster as their humble living quarters.
你把纸条上的数字改成了<unbreak>84</unbreak>——这么做或许只是一时兴起。但是，冥冥之中你有种预感，这改变了很多事情。有一个包裹将消失在地底，有一只铁瓶将坠落，有一个人将哭泣。

You changed the number on this piece of paper to <unbreak>84</unbreak>. You may have done this out of no reason, but you have a feeling that this has changed many things. A package will be forever lost in the underground. An iron bottle will fall. Someone will cry.
预感并不作数，但你也许会为这个小小的恶作剧惴惴不安，悲哀的是，其后发生的事你无从见证。
This is merely your feeling, which does not represent reality, but you do feel a little uneasy because of your tiny prank. Regrettably, you cannot witness the consequences.
你离开这座垃圾箱，将它抛之脑后，不再理睬。
You leave this dumpster and forget all about it.
你把纸条上的数字改成了<unbreak>84</unbreak>——这么做或许只是一时兴起。但是，冥冥之中你有种预感，这改变了很多事情。有一个包裹将消失在地底，有一只铁瓶将坠落，有一个人将哭泣。

You changed the number on this piece of paper to <unbreak>84</unbreak>. You may have done this out of no reason, but you have a feeling that this has changed many things. A package will be forever lost in the underground. An iron bottle will fall. Someone will cry.
预感并不作数，但你也许会为这个小小的恶作剧惴惴不安，悲哀的是，其后发生的事你无从见证。

你离开这座垃圾箱，将它抛之脑后，不再理睬。
You leave this dumpster and forget all about it.
垃圾箱荧荧发光，有人往里面扔了三块地髓，力道很大，以至于矿石碎屑散落得到处都是。你不知道那人的愤怒从何而来，毕竟，地髓就相当于地底的钱。你会在生气的时候扔钱吗？

The dumpster is shining. Someone threw three pieces of Geomarrow in it. The person threw it so heavily that shards of the ore scattered all over the trashcan. You have no idea where the anger of that person stems from, neither can you understand this behavior. After all, Geomarrow is as valuable as gold in the underground. Would you throw money even in a fit of anger?
没错！好了，这个话题就到此为止吧，关于垃圾箱和钱，真的没有什么可说的。
That's right! Now, let's end the conversation here. It's really boring to talk about dumpsters and money.
这座垃圾桶猛一瞧没有什么特别的，但当你细看时，就发现了它的邪恶之处：用来支撑箱盖的弹簧杆被动了手脚。如果有人用力推开箱盖，盖子就会反弹，给那人一点苦头尝尝。

This trashcan looks just like the other ones at first glance, but when you take a closer look, you uncover its sinister nature: Someone has done something to the spring bar of the lid. If you push the lid of this trashcan, the lid will be bounce back to you, dealing a heavy blow.
这花了你一点时间，但结果是好的：你成功修复了弹簧杆，现在不会再有人被垃圾箱伤害了。
You spent some time on it but finally succeeded. Now the spring bar is fixed and nobody will be hurt by this trashcan.
真的？哇，这可真的是非常邪恶的恶作剧，崇高道德会对此表达它的鄙夷。通俗地说，你必须消耗一份崇高道德的赞许才能抵消它，可以吗？
Are you serious? Oh my, this is a truly evil trick. High Morals would despise such behavior. You have to consume a Praise of High Morals to recompense for it. Are you willing to?
崇高道德很伤心，但你的手段确实不赖：现在，当盖子反弹时，箱底的两包菜油就会疯狂喷涌。那个倒霉蛋得额外再洗一件衣服了。阿哈会很喜欢你的恶作剧的。

The High Morals are sad, but admittedly, your trick is quite brilliant. Now, when the lid bounces back, The two packs of oil will be split all over the unlucky person's clothes. Aha would applaud such a prank.
你很想搞点恶作剧，但你不具备这样的条件：就是说，你甚至没有得到过崇高道德的赞许，在这种情况下做坏事，它会很不高兴的。

You want to conduct something so mischievous, but you are unable to do it. Or you can put it that way: You have never received the Praise of High Morals. In that case, if you insist on doing bad things, it will be really upset.
你离开这座垃圾箱，将它抛之脑后，不再理睬。
You leave this dumpster and forget all about it.
你离开这座垃圾箱，将它抛之脑后，不再理睬。
You leave this dumpster and forget all about it.
大垃圾箱肆无忌惮地敞着口，你看见它的肚子里装满了镇民丢弃的垃圾。突然，有个声音开口说话了：「开拓者啊，你想要的是这七个普通的垃圾，还是这个好看的垃圾呢？」

The big trashcan lies in plain air. You can see that it has been filled with citizens' discarded trash. Suddenly, you hear a voice saying: "Trailblazer, do you want these seven normal pieces of trash or this beautiful piece of trash?"
「你是个诚实的人。」那个声音赞许地说，「我将给你你想要的。」
"You are an honest person," the voice says in an approving way, "I will give you what you want."
「你是个诚实的人。」那个声音赞许地说，「我将给你你想要的。」
"You are an honest person," the voice says in an approving way, "I will give you what you want."
「你是个贪婪的人。」那个声音赞许地说，「我将给你你想要的。」
"You are a greedy person," the voice says in an approving way, "I will give you what you want."
「你是个淳朴的人。」那个声音赞许地说，「我将给你你想要的。」
"You are a simple person," the voice says in an approving way, "I will give you what you want."
玩个小游戏。你猜：贝洛伯格的地上有多少个垃圾桶？注意：并排放在一起的只算1个，而且，那些你看得见但够不着的不算。
Game time. Guess: How many trashcans are there in Belobog? Attention: Trashcans put side-by-side together are considered as one. But, those that you can see but cannot be reached do not count.
真棒！——但说实话，不知道你答对了没有。毕竟这里是地下，这个垃圾桶已经很久没见过上面的亲戚了。这样吧，问一个确定有答案的问题！

Nice answer! Honestly, it is uncertain if your answer is correct. This is the Underworld, after all. This trashcan has not seen its Overworld relatives in a long time. You know what, here comes a question with a definite answer!
你猜这个镇子上有多少个垃圾桶？规则照旧：并排放在一起的只算1个，而且，那些你看得见但够不着的不算。对了，垃圾箱也不算。只算这种圆圆的，筒状的垃圾桶。

Guess: How many trashcans are there in this town? The rules are the same: Trashcans placed side-by-side are considered as one. But, those that you can see but cannot be reached do not count. Count only these: Cylindrical trashcans.
哇！你是真的很关心每一个能互动的垃圾桶…答对了！5个是正确答案！这只垃圾桶因为你对它的重视而快乐，就连它体内的垃圾都变得美丽了起来。

Wow! You really do take note of each interactable trashcan... You are correct! Five is the correct answer! This trashcan brims with happiness because of your attention, even the garbage inside it is becoming more beautiful.
错了，但情有可原，谁会没事去数垃圾桶的数量呢？因此，这个游戏就到此为止吧，太幼稚了！它太幼稚了！
This is the wrong answer, but it is understandable. Who would count the number of existing trashcans? So, let's stop this game right now. It's too childish! Childish!
错了，但情有可原，谁会没事去数垃圾桶的数量呢？因此，这个游戏就到此为止吧，太幼稚了！它太幼稚了！
This is the wrong answer, but it is understandable. Who would count the number of existing trashcans? So, let's stop this game right now. It's too childish! Childish!
How would I know? This is not fair!
垃圾桶认为你说得对，它同意这确实不公平，谁会没事去数垃圾桶的数量呢？因此，这个游戏就到此为止吧，太幼稚了！它太幼稚了！
The trashcan thinks you're right, this isn't fair. Who would count the number of existing trashcans? So, let's stop this game right now. It's too childish! Childish!
这是一个垃圾桶，顶盖周圈似乎被人用锐器割开了。
This is a trashcan, its lid seems to have been cut open with some kind of implement.
你打开了铁桶，一股柴薪燃烧特有的焦味携卷金属冷冽的涩味扑面而来，使你一时窒息。向深不见底的桶底望去，只见无垠的黑暗中，点点未燃尽的薪火在其间闪烁流动——它们还不想早早熄灭。

You open the tin lid and a waft of burnt wood mixed with cold steel invades your olfactory senses. Looking into the depth of the trashcan, you can see flickers of embers still hanging on, struggling to extend their own lifespan.
只是一个被切开脑袋的垃圾桶罢了——这种事宇宙里可多的是。「何必和垃圾桶较劲呢？」你如是想着，同伙伴一起离开了。
It is just a lobotomized trashcan, there are many of them across the universe. "What do I have against trashcans?" You find yourself thinking before leaving with your comrades.
经过这只可怜的邮筒时，你在余光中捕捉到了不属于它的一部分——那是泛黄信封的小小一角，有气无力地挂在了筒口边缘。
Passing by this pitiful mailbox, you catch a glimmer of what does not belong — the corner of a yellowing envelope hanging limply on the edge of its opening.
你成功挽救了这个有些脆弱的信封——这可不是一种修辞，而是信封真的在掉渣。纸屑随着矿区特有的烟尘随风而逝，消失在了灼热空气的深处。在彻底化为齑粉之前，你的义举拯救了它。

You rescue the fragile envelope — this is not a hyperbole, the envelope is disintegrating. The paper scraps crumble into thin air, merging with the unique mine dust, then disappear into the abyss of scorching air. Before its final act of glory, your actions gave it salvation.
你将指腹抵在信封上轻轻施力，便将其推入了邮筒深处的无边混沌与黑暗，接着再没有了声音。它就像坠入深海的石子，入水的那一刻起，便与此世的一切再无关联了。

You lightly press against the envelope and push it into the dark and unknowable depths of the mailbox. Like a pebble dropped into the ocean, it lost connection with the outside world the moment it vanished into the abyss.
I have bigger fish to fry.
你仿佛听到内心深处传来一个阴沉的声音：「何必浪费时间？」随后，另一个甜蜜的声音报以回应：「是啊是啊！」你不知道声音来自何方，但他们无疑很快达成了共识，并帮你做了决定。

You hear a deep voice resonating deep within your heart. "Why waste the effort?" Another voice follows, "That's right!" You don't know where the voices come from, but they undoubtedly arrived at the same conclusion and made the decision for you.
这里是星际和平播报，观众朋友们晚上好。
Welcome to the Interastral Peace Broadcast. Good evening everyone.
「存护」克里珀的巨锤徐徐下落，锤声响彻寰宇，震耳欲聋。
As Qlipoth the Preservation's giant hammer slowly descends, its strike echoes among the stars with a deafening clamor.
博识学会星空生态学派宣布：琥珀历<unbreak>2157</unbreak>纪结束，<unbreak>2158</unbreak>纪正式到来。
The Intelligentsia Guild's Astral Ecology School has declared the official end of <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE and the start of <unbreak>2158</unbreak> AE.
在新的纪元里，星际和平公司将一如既往地致力于银河间的和平共处，贸易繁荣。
In this new Era, the Interastral Peace Corporation will continue to wholeheartedly support the peaceful co-existence and commercial prosperity of all residents in the galaxy.
公司将不忘初心，牢记创业之初的唯一原则：将一切奉献给「琥珀王」。
The IPC will remain true to its sole creed upon which it was founded — To dedicate everything to the Amber Lord.
下面让我们回顾在<unbreak>2157</unbreak>琥珀纪发生了哪些重大事件：
Let's take a moment to look back at some of the biggest happenings in <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE.
自<unbreak>2147</unbreak>纪开始出现的神秘现象「星核」造成的污染仍在继续。「星核」阻塞了文明之间的道路，令一度团结在「琥珀王」命途下的诸界分崩离析。

The mysterious phenomenon known as Stellaron corrosion continues to propagate. Stellarons have blocked travel between civilizations since <unbreak>2147</unbreak> AE, and they shattered the union of the worlds that were once united under the Path of the Amber Lord.
漫长的十纪中，星际和平公司从未放弃将诸界重新连通的愿景。在博识学会的鼎力支持下，我们已初步掌握了绕过星核污染的技术，并在一百一十三个星系进行了实验，取得了可喜的成果。

Over the past ten long eras, the Interastral Peace Corporation had never given up on our hope of reconnecting the worlds. With the Intelligentsia Guild's ardent support, we have established preliminary technologies to bypass the Stellaron's corrosion, and have obtained positive results after experimenting on <unbreak>113</unbreak> star systems.
漫长的十纪中，星际和平公司从未放弃将诸界重新连通的愿景。
For the entirety of the past ten Amber Eras, the Interastral Peace Corporation has never given up on our hope of reconnecting the worlds.
在博识学会的鼎力支持下，我们已初步掌握了绕过星核污染的技术，并在一百一十三个星系进行了实验，取得了可喜的成果。

With the Intelligentsia Guild's ardent support, we have begun developing technologies to bypass Stellaron Corrosion. Experiments conducted across <unbreak>113</unbreak> galaxies have shown promising results.
银河浩瀚无边，而我们并不孤单。已陨的星神，「开拓」阿基维利曾搭乘的星穹列车如今重新起航。
The galaxy is vast, yet we are not alone. The Astral Express, once piloted by the fallen Aeon Akivili the Trailblaze, has embarked on its journey once again.
这群年轻的无名客自称继承了阿基维利的意志，将重新开辟星与星之间的航线，再现九百九十纪前的「第一次繁荣」。
These young Nameless claim to have inherited the will of Akivili and will re-establish the access routes between the stars, recreating the First Prosperity from <unbreak>990</unbreak> Amber Eras ago.
在此，我们代表星际和平公司向星穹列车的开拓者们送上最真挚的祝福。
On behalf of the Interastral Peace Corporation, we wish the best of luck to the Trailblazers of the Astral Express.
在寰宇人民深受星核困扰的同时，一伙新兴起的邪恶组织「星核猎手」却在宇宙中四处作乱。
While the residents of the galaxy are plagued by the Stellarons, a new evil organization called the Stellaron Hunters has been stirring up trouble.
此前报导过，「星核猎手」入侵了天才俱乐部的螺丝咕姆星球，与星球的主人螺丝咕姆爆发了冲突。根据最新消息，他们上一次出现的地点是仙舟「罗浮」。

We have previously reported that the Stellaron Hunters invaded Planet Screwllum of the Genius Society and started a conflict with the owner of that planet, Screwllum. According to the latest reports, the Stellaron Hunters were most recently sighted at the Xianzhou Luofu.
公司驻联盟特派员已向罗浮将军景元求证，截至目前尚未得到正式回复。
The IPC's Special Commissioner to the Alliance had contacted General Jing Yuan of the Luofu to verify this information. We have yet to receive a response.
「星核猎手」曾犯下多起大案，任何一名成员均有独自覆灭一颗星球的能力，其效忠的星神尚不明确，划定为极度危险份子。

The Stellaron Hunters have committed many significant crimes. Any one of their members has the power to destroy a planet, and it remains uncertain which Aeon the group is loyal to. They are classified as extremely dangerous terrorists.
如遭遇疑似星核猎手的成员，请立刻联系公司，切勿打草惊蛇。提供有效线索者，将获得重金酬赏。
If you encounter anyone suspected of being a Stellaron Hunter, please contact the IPC immediately and do not act on your own. Anyone who provides useful information will be substantially rewarded.
此前报导过，「星核猎手」入侵了天才俱乐部的螺丝咕姆星球，与星球的主人螺丝咕姆爆发了冲突。

As we previously discussed on our program, the Stellaron Hunters illegally gained access to Planet Screwllum, which is under the purview of the Genius Society, and clashed with the head of the planet, Screwllum.
根据最新消息，他们上一次出现的地点是仙舟「罗浮」。公司驻联盟特派员已向罗浮将军景元求证，截至目前尚未得到正式回复。

Our latest reports suggest that the criminal group has recently been spotted on the Xianzhou Luofu. The IPC's Special Commissioner to the Alliance has contacted General Jing Yuan of the Luofu to verify this information. We have yet to receive a response.
已知多个派系收到了盛会之星「匹诺康尼」的邀请函。据悉，这是匹诺康尼的「家族」首次公开向银河各派系发出正式邀约。

Meanwhile, many factions have received an invitation to Penacony, the Planet of Festivities. This is the first time that The Family in Penacony has openly sent formal invitations to all galactic factions.
星际和平公司也在邀请之列，「战略投资部」部长钻石称将谨慎评估家族此举的意义，并将积极友好作出应对。

The Interastral Peace Corporation is among the factions invited. The head of the Strategic Investment Department, Diamond, has stated that we will carefully examine the purpose behind this gesture, and respond to it in a positive and friendly manner.
我们期盼银河各方携手并肩，守护美好家园，抵御邪恶势力的侵犯。
We look forward to having all galactic factions join together to protect our homelands and defend against the invasion of evil forces.
「绝灭大君」焚风现身于提亚奴阿星系。据悲悼伶人的消息，该星系已遭毁灭。
The Lord Ravager, Zephyro, was spotted at the Tia'nua galaxy. According to the Mourning Actors, that galaxy has been completely destroyed.
反物质军团正在全银河范围内肆虐，引发的灾变与破坏难以数计。公司提请广大群众重视「存护」的意义。
The Antimatter Legion is ravaging through the galaxy and has caused countless calamities. The IPC would like to remind everyone to remember the significance of the Preservation.
任何接入心灵通感信标的星球，都可以向公司免费索取由筑城者提供的《星际和平指南》。
Any planet with a telepathy beacon can obtain from the IPC free copies of the Interastral Peace Guide written by the Architects.
在琥珀<unbreak>2158</unbreak>纪的开端，星际和平公司骄傲地宣布：天才俱乐部著名学者黑塔女士已正式承诺与公司达成长期友好合作同盟关系。

At the beginning of <unbreak>2158</unbreak> AE, the Interastral Peace Corporation would like to proudly announce that the famous scientist, Madam Herta of the Genius Society, has officially committed to a long-term alliance and cooperative relationship with the IPC.
这将是第<unbreak>22</unbreak>号会员利尔他、第<unbreak>56</unbreak>号会员以利亚萨拉斯之后，第三位智识的天才与公司携手，共同存护琥珀王的宇宙。

Madam Herta is the third genius of the Erudition to partner with the IPC, after Member #22 Aiden and Member #56 Elias Salas. The Corporation stands united with Madam Herta to protect the Amber Lord's universe.
本次播报到此结束，请在指定时间收听下一周期的星际和平播报。再见。
And this is the end of our broadcast. Please tune in for our next episode. Thank you for listening.

Welcome to a new episode of Interastral Peace Broadcast. We are here to bring you the latest news from across the galaxy.
冰封世界雅利洛-Ⅵ重新传来通讯信号，星球原住民或有生还可能。
Signal transmissions from Jarilo-VI have been detected. This could mean that there are still survivors on the icy planet.
「厄兆先锋」向西格玛叁号星系发出警告，恶性噬硅细菌存在向该地区传播趋势。
The Omen Vanguards have warned the Sigmar-III galaxy about the potential spread of malignant silicon-eating bacteria.
博识学会武装考古学派在海尔雷星发现一微缩星系，相关工程正在持续进行中。
The Armed Archaeologists of the Intelligentsia Guild have discovered a miniature galaxy on Hellem. Further investigation is being conducted.
「荷尔斯泰因大静默」被证实是「焚化工」所为，巡海游侠宣布正在追查失窃记忆下落。

It has been confirmed that The Cremators are responsible for the Holstein Aphasia. Galaxy Rangers have announced that they are working on retrieving stolen memories and that the investigation is ongoing.
以下为您展开详细报道。
Let's take a closer look at the details.
日前，据星际和平家电雅利洛片区分店某销售员消息称，冰封七百余年的雅利洛-Ⅵ表面再度传来通讯信号。

A sales rep from the Jarilo branch of Interastral Peace Appliances told us that signal transmissions have been detected from Jarilo-VI, a planet that has been sealed under heavy snow for over <unbreak>700</unbreak> years.
公司对此高度重视，第一时间对该信号进行了深度解析。
The IPC responded promptly and performed an analysis on the signals.
根据信号内容，雅利洛-Ⅵ原住民在数琥珀纪前的浩劫中幸存至今，并于不久前在一群无名客的帮助下成功结束了大寒潮。

The message that the signals contain states that the natives of the planet have survived the catastrophe that happened many Amber Eras ago and recently received help from a group known as the Nameless, which allowed them to put an end to the Eternal Freeze.
目前，公司方面已派出相关人员前往调查雅利洛-Ⅵ现状。让我们祝愿幸存者们尽快回归「存护」的命途！
The IPC has sent out investigators to assess the situation on Jarilo-VI. Let us hope that the survivors can return to the Path of Preservation as soon as possible!
「厄兆先锋」于近日同星际和平公司取得联系，向公司预警了即将来临的灾变。
The Omen Vanguards recently reached out to the IPC and warned about the spread of a certain strain of bacteria.
该派系成员委托公司向西格玛叁号星系发出警告，在柔贰号星系大规模蔓延的恶性噬硅细菌存在向该星区传播的趋势。

Members from the Vanguards have asked the IPC to alert the residents that there are signs of an outbreak of a malignant silicon-eating bacteria in Sigmar-III. The bacteria had previously been seen proliferating in Mollis-II.
据博识学会有关专家表示，此种恶性细菌可以通过剧烈的生化反应，将绝大部分硅酸盐及硅氧化合物中的硅原子剥离，并供给其自我复制。

Experts from the Intelligentsia Guild suggest that this type of bacteria can peel off the silicon atoms stored in the vast majority of silicates and silicone compounds via violent biochemical reactions. It will then use these silicon atoms to duplicate itself.
藉由此种生物过程，该细菌会分泌一种具有强腐蚀性的生物膜，若处理不当，将对人身财产安全造成严重威胁。
In this process, the bacteria will generate a membrane that is highly corrosive. When not handled properly, this type of bacteria can pose a serious hazard to health and safety.
星际和平公司在此呼吁：该星系的机械生命领导人应尽早尽快制定应对政策，防止人身财产安全蒙受损失，并遏制疾病的进一步蔓延。
The IPC calls upon the leaders of mechanical life forms in Sigmar-III to take action to contain the bacteria and safeguard public health.
博识学会武装考古学派话事人在近期的新闻发布会上表示，海尔雷星分队在星球南回归线某洞窟中发现了一微缩星系。

The spokesperson of the Armed Archaeologists under the Intelligentsia Guild stated in a recent press conference that a squad dispatched to Hellem has discovered a miniature galaxy in a cave located near the planet's southern tropical latitudes.
据称，该分队在当地山脉开展爆破工程时，在剖开的山体中意外发现一容器样建筑。调查过后，该分队确信其中存在一虚数亚空间场，并确认了其功能。

The squad was conducting demolition missions on certain mountain ranges when they discovered a curious facility in the shape of a container. After investigation, the squad was convinced that there was a Sub-space field within the facility and confirmed its function.
海尔雷分队队长向公司介绍，该亚空间场实际上是一微缩星系，且该人造星系中很可能仍存有居民。
The squad leader told the IPC that the sub-space field they had discovered was in fact a miniature artificially made galaxy and that they were fairly certain that it was still populated.
目前，海尔雷分队正在进一步持续开展相关工程，试图与亚空间场中的居民取得联系。星际和平播报将对此事进行持续报道。
As of now, the squad has been carrying out further work in an attempt to contact the galaxy's residents. We will bring you more updates on this developing story as they become available.
某不愿透露姓名的巡海游侠声称，琥珀<unbreak>2157</unbreak>纪初期的「荷尔斯泰因大静默」实由「焚化工」所为。
A member of the Galaxy Rangers who wishes to remain anonymous claims that The Cremators are behind the Holstein Aphasia that occurred in early <unbreak>2157</unbreak> AE.
据可靠消息，小股不法分子通过某种特殊手段，将荷尔斯泰因星系全部居民有关「语言」的记忆全部窃走。
Research from reliable sources suggest that certain criminal groups snatched all the memories relating to "language" from the residents of Holstein via unconventional means.
因此，失去了「语言」记忆的荷尔斯泰因居民忘记了如何说话，导致了该星系全域静默至今。
The residents of Holstein have since lost the ability to produce speech. To this day, the Holstein galaxy remains silent.
在星际和平公司的帮助下，该星系已可通过书面通用语进行简单沟通。
There is some good news, though — with the help of the IPC, the residents of Holstein have learned to communicate through written language.
本次播报到此结束，请在指定时间收听下一周期的星际和平播报。再见。
And this is the end of our broadcast. Please tune in for our next episode. Thank you for listening.
